action.reasoning: Observation: test_prep confirms ~7k samples with bbox/point targets. Train fails at first step with split_with_sizes RuntimeError (sum split_sizes=[1664] != tensor dim2=1) due to incorrect tensor shapes from Dataset (pixel_values[0] slices away all but 1 patch instead of keeping (num_patches,3,h,w); image_grid_thw and input_ids mishandled; likely causes bad qkv lengths in Qwen2VLAttention). Predict.py truncated (incomplete point/JSON write). Persistent prior token/feature mismatches from template/image handling. Analysis: Qwen2VL requires exact pixel_values shape (num_patches not batched), image_grid_thw=(1,3)->squeeze(0), input_ids/attention_mask squeezed but labels masked via assistant header search; passing PIL image in messages + single processor call aligns pads (416 after 2x2 merge on 1664 patches). Smaller max_pixels + correct shapes will fix error and enable training. Decision: Overwrite train.py (full correct Dataset with PIL in msgs, proper no-slice on pixel_values, bs=1 manual loop, metrics logging, 128 samples/32 steps, save adapter+processor+grid) and complete predict.py (full parse/rescale/clip/default/JSON per contract). Run test_prep+train then submit_eval for first grounding_score + time baseline before scaling data/epochs/LR.
